items.title: Nine weeks of rolling phone, internet outages in remote NT community

children.content: Wadeye, one of Australia's largest Aboriginal communities, has been without a stable mobile phone service for months, leaving locals unable to make calls or access the internet.

gallery.altText: An aerial shot of a remote NT community, with homes scattered alongside red dirt roads.

thumbnail.altText: An aerial shot of a remote NT community, with homes scattered alongside red dirt roads.

items.title: Gambling reforms bill slammed as 'kneejerk' response to Four Corners

children.content: The Northern Territory government has introduced legislation intended to reform Australia's de facto online gambling regulator, but it's been criticised as a "damage-control exercise".

image.altText: A person holding a mobile phone in their hand, with a sports betting app open on the phone.

items.title: Woman's 'suspicious' death investigated after car allegedly hit by kangaroo

children.content: NT Police are investigating the "suspicious death" of a woman on a remote stretch of highway in the early hours of Easter morning.

image.altText: An empty highway on a remote landscape, with red dirt meeting the edge of the bitumen.

items.title: Uluru's popular Field of Light installation extended for three years

children.content: Bruce Munro's  art installation is inspired by Uluru. Ten years later, it remains one of the most popular attractions at the famous landmark.

gallery.altText: The camera looks over a mans shoulder onto the moonlit night scene of the Field of Lights glowing.
The silhouette of Uluru hugs the horizon and patches of bright coloured light spreads out before it in a beautiful scene
A tree half blocks the moon and people view glowing flower like orbs of light covering the ground.

thumbnail.altText: The camera looks over a mans shoulder onto the moonlit night scene of the Field of Lights glowing.
The silhouette of Uluru hugs the horizon and patches of bright coloured light spreads out before it in a beautiful scene
A tree half blocks the moon and people view glowing flower like orbs of light covering the ground.

items.title: Vet helping native bat pups has 'never seen mass casualties like this'

children.content: Rescuers are scrambling to save as many flying fox pups as they can, with hundreds of the native bats dead or dying in Top End streets.

image.altText: A volunteer feeds a rescued baby flying fox, which is swaddled in a colourful blanket.

items.title: 'Easter bilby' populations booming across sanctuaries

children.content: The Australian Wildlife Conservancy says the threatened species has rebounded at multiple sites across the country.

image.altText: A small marsupial with its ears stuck upwards, standing on red dirt

items.title: After NT spent 'ridiculous' millions on aircraft, operations move offshore

children.content: A modern version of a 1960s-era aircraft that can land on sea and snow was meant to be built in NSW, and then in the NT. But years later it still hasn’t taken off.

image.altText: A blue plane over the map of the Australia, and images  of Darwin City

items.title: Magnitude-5.5 earthquake 'morning wake-up call' for remote NT communities

children.content: A remote community in the APY Lands have woken up to rattling shelves and swinging lights caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake, Geoscience Australia says.

image.altText: A red dirt football oval next to a grassed football oval and a small community with a mountain range in the background

articles.title: Gunfight outside Israeli consulate in Istanbul leaves attacker dead

children.content: A gunman has been killed and two people injured after an attack outside the Israeli consulate in Türkiye.

image.altText: Police wearing camouflage hold up police tape for an officer to walk through.

children.content: The US president took to Truth Social to issue his latest threat to Tehran. Follow live.

updates.title: Erdogan condemns 'treacherous attack' at Israeli consulate in Istanbul
Iran sets conditions for peace talks, senior official says
Trump: 'A whole civilisation will die tonight'

items.title: Coke bottles to a 200L drum: Petrol stations see dangerous fuel stockpiling

children.content: Authorities are warning that improper or excessive storage of petrol or diesel in suburban homes carries a significant fire risk, as service station owners report excessive stockpiling.

image.altText: man in petorl station holding pump

items.title: Tributes for Aboriginal health leader and The Sapphires singer

children.content: Dr Naomi Mayers helped establish the first Aboriginal medical services in Australia, turning self-determination into a model of care across the country. Her death came days before Wiradjuri woman and "fierce trailblazer" Ann Weldon.

image.altText: A red brick building with a man walking through a door.

items.title: Artemis II reveals previously unseen views of far side of the Moon

children.content: The astronauts have travelled further from Earth than any humans before, capturing never-before-seen views of the far side of the Moon.

gallery.altText: A male astronaut's head is seen in profile, looking back at Earth through a spacecraft cabin window.

thumbnail.altText: A male astronaut's head is seen in profile, looking back at Earth through a spacecraft cabin window.
AN image of the Moon, as seen from space.
Earth's Moon, as seen from a spacecraft.

items.title: Ex-Bondi nanny fails in bid to avoid extradition over Chilean kidnapping claims

children.content: A former Bondi nanny accused of torture and kidnapping for the Chilean Pinochet regime loses her final legal bid to avoid extradition to Chile.

image.altText: A woman with glasses.

items.title: Rohan Dennis speaks publicly for the first time since Melissa Hoskins's death

children.content: Former professional cyclist Rohan Dennis has spoken publicly for the first time about the death of his wife Melissa Hoskins, saying he and his children have since been "harassed" and "stalked" by the media.

image.altText: Rohan Dennis wearing formal attire walks along a sandstone wall, with a broadcast camera partially visible.

items.title: Townsville victim counts cost of crime spree that saw nine arrested

children.content: Daniel Toppi was just starting to get back on his feet before a group of teenagers allegedly stole his work vehicle. Police have arrested nine teenagers in relation to the incident that also included the alleged theft of a police vehicle.

image.altText: A thin middle aged man with dark hair and a somber expression.

items.title: Pheobe Bishop murder-accused to face court on anniversary of 17yo's disappearance

children.content: The case of a woman accused of murdering Queensland teenager Pheobe Bishop has been adjourned to May 15 — the first anniversary of Pheobe's disappearance.

image.altText: A close up photo of a woman with red hair

items.title: Vegan leather sounds eco‑friendly but the truth is complex

children.content: As fashion brands move away from animal leather, "vegan" has come to suggest something that is both kinder to animals and better for the planet. However, the reality is more complicated.

gallery.altText: Man with arm bend at elbow wearing a worn brown leather jacket with a mural on the wall behind

thumbnail.altText: Man with arm bend at elbow wearing a worn brown leather jacket with a mural on the wall behind

items.title: I am tired of constantly defending being single and child-free

children.content: I might be on a different path, but I can still love and be loved in ways that don't require me to be a mother or a partner.

image.altText: Julie stands in a green dress, smiling.

items.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world are we?

children.content: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

image.altText: A toy rabbit sits in a field of tulips

items.title: 'MAGA for Mamdani': The issue flipping Trump voters to a left-wing Democrat

children.content: US President Donald Trump and New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani are from polar opposite ends of the political spectrum, but comic Ron Barba voted for them both.

gallery.altText: Zohran Mamdani raises his right hand as he takes an oath

thumbnail.altText: Zohran Mamdani raises his right hand as he takes an oath

items.title: Trump threatens 'demolition' of Iran and takes another swipe at Australia

children.content: Donald Trump says the "entire country" of Iran will be "taken out" if no deal is reached by Wednesday morning (AEST), ratcheting up his threat to target civilian infrastructure and brushing off concerns it would be a war crime.

image.altText: Donald Trump speaks at a podium.

items.title: Trump threatens to jail journalist who broke story of US rescue mission in Iran

children.content: US President Donald Trump is threatening to jail the journalist who broke the story about the rescue of a downed US airman in Iran, as more details about the daring recovery mission are made public.

image.altText: black wreckage in an open landscape with mountains in the background

items.title: 'They are torturing the public': Inside India's cooking gas crisis

children.content: Across Delhi, people are waiting days and sometimes weeks for gas cylinders. Yet, the ABC has found a thriving black market, where the same gas can be bought quickly — for those who can afford it.

gallery.altText: A group of people line up against a wall in Delhi.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people line up against a wall in Delhi.

items.title: 'Grow up': Meta censoring warnings about illicit drugs, health experts say

children.content: Public health workers are warning lives could be at risk due to social media sites automatically censoring educational posts about illicit drugs circulating in the community.

image.altText: a small orange and grey pill next to a ruler

items.title: Man jailed for role in major steroid and prescription drug syndicate

children.content: A Canberra man is jailed for his part in an illegal drug ring which operated as an online pharmacy, distributing steroids and prescription drugs across the country.

image.altText: A box of pills and drugs.

items.title: Rapper Offset hospitalised after shooting in Florida

children.content: The Migos rapper is in a stable condition though his exact condition is unknown, according to his spokesperson.

image.altText: A man poses with glasses and a suit.

items.title: Man sent home from hospital with '10/10' pain ends up losing chunk of leg

children.content: An Australian Navy candidate says he nearly lost his leg following delays in diagnosis and treatment of his emergency condition by two Perth hospitals.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a man's leg with a large scar running down his shin and a brace hanging down the side as he sits on a bench.

items.title: Tech starts to eat itself as AI reshapes workforce

children.content: The wave of change from artificial intelligence rolling across the corporate landscape is going largely unnoticed as attention understandably shifts to an escalating war.

gallery.altText: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone.

thumbnail.altText: A silhouette of a person looking at a phone.

items.title: Broncos name Walsh replacement, Rooster returns from 10-match suspension

children.content: Jesse Arthars has been named at fullback for the Brisbane Broncos ahead of Friday's blockbuster clash against the North Queensland Cowboys.

image.altText: NRL player running with the ball at training, with a teammate in support

items.title: Moloney knocks back $250k to lock in world title fight

children.content: Australian boxer Andrew Moloney will fight Willibaldo Garcia for the IBF super-flyweight championship.

image.altText: Boxer yells with excitement at the end of a fight, standing in the ring

items.title: Pro-climate son-in-law to Rupert Murdoch backs Farrer independent

children.content: Backers of Farrer independent candidate Michelle Milthorpe are celebrating after securing the support of a prominent pro-climate farmer who is also the son-in-law of media mogul Rupert Murdoch.

gallery.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

thumbnail.altText: Michelle Milthorpe talks to two people with their backs to the camera in the bistro of a pub.

items.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila likely to cross Far North Queensland

children.content: The Bureau of Meteorology is increasingly confident the system will move towards the Queensland coast later in the week, potentially crossing Cape York Peninsula.

image.altText: A satellite image of a large amount of cloud cover in a circle on a map

items.title: Fears for Ningaloo Reef after ex-cyclone leaves animals strewn along beaches

children.content: As sharks swarm in the aftermath, there are fears ex-tropical Cyclone Narelle may have triggered a climate disaster for the world-famous Ningaloo Reef.

image.altText: A dead dolphin on a beach, with debris washed up behind it.

items.title: Israeli settler violence in the West Bank deepens against backdrop of Iran war

children.content: Under the cover of regional conflict, Israelis who have established illegal settlements and outposts in the West Bank become emboldened to take over more land as world attention remains on the war in Iran.

image.altText: Mourners sit outside a hospital morgue in the West Bank.

items.title: How China uses ferries and fishing boats to pressure Taiwan

children.content: China has quietly mobilised thousands of fishing boats twice in recent months to form floating barriers at least 300 kilometres long.

image.altText: A graphic features fishing vessels, a target and maps featuring the flags of China and Taiwan.
